observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and all the responsible officials to:

immediately submit the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents, and cancelled checks, to support the recorded check disbursements. This is without prejudice to any notices of suspension, charges or disallowances that may be issued as a result of our audit. Henceforth, ensure that all disbursement vouchers, pertinent supporting documents and cancelled checks be submitted on a regular and timely basis to the audit team.
observations.reason: No proof of compliance submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

require the officials and employees concerned to create and maintain an LGU website and cause the immediate posting of the report thereon, and also in at least three conspicuous public places in the locality;
observations.reason: No proof of compliance submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 

formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Audit Team; copy furnished the DBM, the Speaker of the House of the Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 99 of RA 11260.
observations.reason: No submission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

strictly adhere       with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-3 dated May 23, 2017 and require concerned officials to fast track the implementation of the program in order to accomplish the purpose for which the fund was granted and to deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Projects already completed but not yet liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

include the representatives from the NGOs that promotes recycling and protection of air and water quality, recycling industry and manufacturing or packaging industry as members of the MESWMB to tap their expertise which could contribute to the efforts of the municipality in complying the solid waste management rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Management admits impossibility of full  compliance to the subject recommendation even after exhausting all their efforts in searching and identifying NGOs to be a member of the MESWMB as subject representatives are not available in their locality considering that geographically, they are situated in an island.  The LGU made representation to the DILG to accredit various NGOs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC):

submit the lacking documents to support the purchase of the service motorboat. For similar purchase in the ensuing year, see to it that the provisions mandated by DBM National Budget Circular No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998 are strictly followed.
observations.reason: Lacking relevant documents were still not submitted to date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the concerned officers and employees be required to:

immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to: 

maintain subsidiary records of unliquidated cash advances and to exert extra efforts in identifying the particulars/information of the unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: There are still outstanding cash advances as of end of CY 2024  4th Quarter.

observations.recommendation:
We require the concerned accountable officers to: 

immediately settle their suspensions and disallowances in accordance with the 2009 Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.
observations.reason: There are still unsettled suspensions and disallowances.

We recommend that an: 

an inventory of land, land improvements and building be made and the results of the physical count be reconciled with its recorded valuation. 

Further, these properties should be: 

registered in the name of the Municipality with the Register of Deeds and duly supported with the proof of ownership such as certificate of land title and/or declaration of real properties.
observations.reason:
No Inventory made.



No proof of compliance submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
We recommended and the Management agreed ,that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor:
submit justifications/explanations on the unimplemented and delayed development projects; and
expedite the procurement process on the unimplemented development project.

We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor directs the MDRRMO to ensure and expedite the implementation of identified PPAs, to strengthen the agency’s capacity in mitigating and preparing for, responding to and recovering from the impact of disasters.

We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to:
formulate guidelines on the implementation of the Municipality’s financial assistance programs;
submit the said guidelines to the Municipal Mayor for approval; and
submit the approved guidelines to the local sanggunian for adoption.
We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit the overdue withheld taxes.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

require its Collectors to prepare their RCDs; and

turn-over their collections to the designated LO at end of each business day; and

the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

prepare the necessary correcting journal entries to adjust the erroneous booking of PPE accounts.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor: 



stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officers with an existing unsettled previous cash advance.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor: 

initiate the procurement and implementation process to realize the programmed projects as planned; and

ensure that development projects are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready.

We recommended that the LCE:

direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that financial transactions are supported with complete documentation; and

require the BAC to conduct continuous posting of the updated APP, NOAs, and contracts or POs of awarded projects pursuant to Items 3.6 and 9 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, for reporting and monitoring purposes of fund utilization, in relation to the existing COVID-19 pandemic. This audit observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice/s of Suspension, if warranted.

We recommend that management: 

strictly implement the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations RA 9184, Section 4 (6) of PD 1445, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that management: 

direct the Municipal Accountant to process transactions in accordance with governing rules and regulations specifically Section 4 of PD 1445. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance as a result of post-audit.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant does not process the DV if the supporting documents is incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Accountant to:
a. secure from LBP, bank statements and copies of credit memos and debit
memos pertaining to the seven bank accounts;
b. verify the nature and details of the bank accounts;
c. prepare bank reconciliation statements for each bank account; and
d. prepare the necessary journal entries to recognize the seven unrecorded
bank accounts with a total balance of ₱9,048,682.15, including the
transactions that transpired in each of the accounts.

2.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
require:
a. the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
1. regularly update the Real Property Tax Account Register
(RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card (TIC); and
2. furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name
of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year based on
the updated RPTAR/TIC.
b. the Municipal Accountant to establish RPT/SET Receivables at the
beginning of the year based on the duly certified list showing the name of
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as prepared by
the Acting Municipal Treasurer.

3.5 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately send collection letters to the
delinquent taxpayers; and

3.6 We likewise recommended and Management agreed that the Legislative
Officials, in consultation with the Acting Municipal Treasurer and other
concerned officials to draw up plans to strategize and strengthen collection
drive.

4.5 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare the necessary correcting entry to adjust the erroneous booking of
PPE accounts totaling ₱1,332,972.02, net of Accumulated Depreciation,
into its corresponding expenses and equity account; and
b. take into consideration the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000 for
recognition as PPE as provided in Section 4 of the Circular.

5.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. verify the validity of the Accounts Payable aged two years and more. Valid
claims shall be paid, otherwise, cause the reversion of the payables to the
Government Equity account of the General Fund;
b. produce the lacking supporting documents for the current year Accounts
Payable that were not presented; and
c. make the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the
affected accounts in the financial statements.

6.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment entry.

7.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the unrecorded fund
transfers received from DILG and DBM.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Municipal Accountant to compute for the correct tax due on the OCA
of the Municipal Health Workers; and
b. the Acting Municipal Treasurer to withhold and remit the same to the
BIR.

9.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a. immediately realign the subject Intelligence Fund to the LGU’s local
development program (LDP) related PPAs, thru a supplemental budget;
and
b. stop appropriating funds for Intelligence Fund.

10.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a) submit justification on the unimplemented/unfinished projects;
b) initiate the procurement and implementation process to realize the programmed
projects as planned; and
c) ensure that development projects are well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready

11.19 We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require:
a. the PHN-OIC to:
i. record all donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List
of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, duly supported with
the (1) Acknowledgment Receipts, (2) proof of receipt by and
distribution to the beneficiaries not necessarily in the required form, and
(3) the inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any; and
ii. submit a copy of the same to the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant.
b. the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
i. assist the PHN-OIC in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-
in-kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining undistributed
items, if any;
ii. maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed
and Balances and its supporting documents; and
iii. prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.
c. the Municipal Accountant to, based on the Registry of Donated Relief Goods
for DRRM, make a disclosure in the Notes to FS as to the quantity of received,
issued and balance of all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with
categorization as to food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits,
and others.

12.13 We recommend and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer – Designate to:
1. prepare the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan in
order to assess the required number of JO workers to be hired based
on necessity and financial capability of the municipality; and
2. prepare a job description to delineate the specific responsibilities of the
JO workers; 
b. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary deductions for all leave of
absences.

13.15 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a. take full advantage of the utilization of available funds for disaster
preparedness, prevention and mitigation in order to further enhance the
capability of the Municipality to effectively and efficiently prevent losses and
alleviate the impacts of disasters;
b. require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to;
i. enhance the monitoring of LDRRMF utilization focusing on PPAs
requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the
four thematic areas of DRRM; and
ii. ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed
projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of
the fund and to warrant that the goals and objectives of reducing
disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response
capabilities are achieved;
c. require the Municipal Accountant to:
i. initiate the transfer of the unexpended portion of the prior years’
LDRRMF from the GF to TF;
ii. book-up the funds transferred from the GF to TF bank account
pertaining to the unutilized portion of the CY 2016 and prior years’
LDRRMF; and
iii. prepare subsidiary ledgers for the unutilized portion of the CY 2016
and prior years’ LDRRMF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
1. Create an Inventory Committee with adequate number of members and
representatives from the Accounting and Property Division/Unit;
2. Instruct the Inventory Committee to:
a. prioritize the conduct of physical count of PPEs; and
b. prepare the RPCPPE, reconcile the same with the Property
Division/Unit and Accounting Unit records
3. Instruct the Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting
entries, if any, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the LCE: a) direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and maintain the SLCs; b) require the Municipal Treasurer/Supply
Office to keep and maintain SCs; and c) order the Inventory Committee to
conduct the physical count of inventories at year end and to prepare and
submit the RPCI to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of the following
year.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly
and quarterly trial balances, and year-end financial statements to the
audit team on or before the date prescribed by the Commission on
Audit, moving forward.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Bids
and Awards Committee (BAC), Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC), and all others concerned to prepare the
necessary documents for the conduct of bidding. Henceforth, ensure that the
20% DF is promptly and fully utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the BAC Secretariat to
submit the contracts and all the documentary requirements on the
preparatory, advertising and posting, receipt and opening of bids, post-
qualification and awarding of the contracts, for auditorial review and
evaluation in accordance with COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) refrain from recording as outright expenses the purchased/acquired
inventories, but instead, record the same to their appropriate inventories
accounts first; and b) recognize expense only upon issuance, based on RSMI
prepared by the Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer, supported with RIS and
supporting documents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant and all the responsible officials to immediately submit
the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents, and
cancelled checks, to support the recorded check disbursements. This is
without prejudice to any notices of suspension, charges or disallowances that
may be issued as a result of our audit.

We recommend that management transfer the remaining unexpended balance of LDRRMF amounting to P1,770,516.94 to a Special Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014. Likewise, submit the lacking documents to support the disbursements amounting to P348,284.26.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 3 AAR 2014 Finding No.2 AAR 2013 Finding No.7
observations.reason: See related discussion on Finding No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes, and to strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to support the disbursements amounting to P637,509.60 as provided under Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, and Sections 39(1) and 39(3) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 4 AAR 2014 Finding No.5 AAR 2013 Finding No.10 AAR 2012 Finding No.09 AAR 2011 Finding No. 07
observations.reason: See related discussion on Finding No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board be directed to implement the Solid Waste Management Plan pursuant to Sections 12 & 16 of RA 9003.
observations.reason: The Solid Waste Management Plan had been drafted however the said plan remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should undertake the following measures, to wit: 1. Direct the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices to properly maintain property records and to conduct regular reconciliation of both records so that errors could be detected and necessary adjustments be made to come up with correct balances.
observations.reason: See related discussion on Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to process transactions in accordance with governing rules and regulations specifically Section 4 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: DV’s were still to be retrieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the pertinent guidelines embodied in the GPPB Resolution 018-2006 in implementing infrastructure projects thru ’’by administration’’ scheme.
observations.reason: This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance as a result of post-audit.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and the member of the Inventory Committee should make the necessary steps for the proper implementation of RA 656.
We require the concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their suspensions and disallowances in accordance with the 2009 Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to depreciate
the “Road Networks” and submit detailed schedule of PPE. Likewise, require
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the PPE
ledger cards and property cards, respectively. Further, create an Inventory
Committee consisting of two or more employees who shall take charge of the
annual inventory-taking and to submit a report thereon.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to record
purchases of inventory under the perpetual inventory method and maintain
supplies ledger cards for each item as provided for in PPSAS 12. Likewise,
require the employee assigned in the safekeeping of those items to maintain a
complete record of receipts and issuances, and render a report on the Summary
of Supplies and Material Issued.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be
required to submit the accounts and financial reports and to strictly observe
the timely submission of the same in accordance with Section 347 of Republic
Act 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009 and Section 39, PD 1445.

We recommend for a strict observance of the guidelines on the
appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF to avoid unnecessary inclusion of
programs, projects and activities (PPAs) as well as expenditures which are not
related to disaster risk management activities, and to transfer the unexpended
balance of the fund to a Special Trust Fund as provided for in COA Circular No.
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the Special Education Fund (SEF) be fully utilized
and the repair and maintenance for school building be given utmost priority in
the preparation of the Annual School Board Budget as provided for in Section
100© of RA 7160, and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.
1, series of 1998 dated April 14, 1998 in order to provide the students with
facilities that are conducive to learning.

We recommend that management cause the implementation of the
projects funded by 20% Development Fund and in the same manner, limit the
utilization for the purposes that are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit the
lacking documents to support the purchase of the service motorboat. For
similar purchase in the ensuing year, see to it that the provisions mandated
by DBM National Budget Circular No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998 are strictly
followed.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee be directed to
submit copies of approved contracts/purchase order within 5 days after their
execution/issuance as provided for in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to stamp
“PAID” the disbursement vouchers and all their supporting documents after
payment and indicate the number of the check issued and the date of actual
payment to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in payment
of future claims.

We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement
the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished
AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee
on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by
Section 93 of RA 10651.

We recommend that all Collection Clerks be required to strictly observed Section 307 of RA 7160 by having their collections turnover daily to the Municipal Treasurer and for the latter to remit her collections intact to the depository bank. Moreover, require Collection Clerks and Municipal Treasurer to account for the 19 official receipts that were not presented for audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Partially Implemented The Collection Clerks had showed improvements in the remittance of their collections as well as the turnover daily to the Municipal Treasurer in the deposits of her collections to the AAR bank. Moreover, 2013 Finding No.1 AAR 2012 Finding No.2

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officers and employees be required to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records of unliquidated cash advances and to exert extra efforts in identifying the particulars/information of the unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Partially Implemented Finding No. 2 AAR 2014 Finding No.1 AAR 2013 Finding No.1 AAR 2012 Finding No.3 AAR 2007 Finding No.1
observations.reason: There is an apparent effort of the management to lessen the unliquidated cash advances. However, ballooning unliquidated cash advances still exists.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management establish a GAD Focal Point System and ensure its functionality. Likewise, prepare a GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and GAD Accomplishment Reports and submit the same to DILG Regional Office for review and approval. Moreover, see to it that the details or breakdown of the specific programs, projects and activities of each office are stated or clearly identified in the obligation requests, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to ensure that the expenditures are aligned with the GAD Plan. Further, see to it that the expenses are supported with documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 5 AAR 2014 Finding No. 7 AAR 2012 Finding No.11

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management limit the hiring of job order personnel for a duration not exceeding six (6) months. See to it that the hired job order personnel are covered by a contract of service indicating among others, the rate per day, period to render eservices, duties and responsibilities, activities to be performed etc. Likewise, see to it that the payment of their wages shall be supported with the documentary requirements laid out in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 6 AAR 2014 Finding No.8 AAR 2013 Finding No.07 AAR 2012 Finding No. 11

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a Local School Board be created to determine the budgetary needs for the operation and maintenance of public schools within the municipality to be funded out of the Special Education Fund pursuant to Section 98 of RA 7160. Likewise, strictly observe the limitation on the use of Special Education Fund (SEF), as provided for in Section 272, Chapter VII of Republic Act 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 8 AAR 2014 Finding No. 8 AAR 2013 Finding No.4
observations.reason: See related discussion on Finding No. 5 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be required to submit the accounts and financial reports and to strictly observe the timely submission of the same in accordance with Section 347 of Republic Act 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39, PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 9 AAR 2013 Finding No.12 AAR 2012 Finding No.13
observations.reason: See related discussion on Finding No. 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal mayor require the municipal accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to record all reconciling items and adjustments in the books.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Finding No. 3 AAR 2013 Finding No.2
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to make the necessary adjustments to take up the cancelled/staled checks and the corrections of the differences in prior year balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU in coordination with Social Welfare and Development Office formulate policies/guidelines on the grant and submit the same to the Sanggunian for adoption.
observations.reason: Guidelines have been established by the MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management undertake the following remedial measures: strict observance of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, Section 4 (6) of PD 1445, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in procurement.
observations.reason: Various purchases of the LGU were still resorted to reimbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should undertake the following remedial measures: a) adopt the required five (5) days a week supplementary feeding program and prepare separate schedule for each day care centers indicating the number of beneficiaries, duration of the feeding program and the menu for each feeding program and supplementary feeding program for day care centers located outside the municipality; b) see to it that the delivery period for foodstuff shall be made a day before the schedule feeding program for five (5) days consumption; and c) direct the day care workers to conduct constant dialogue with the organized parents informing them the objectives of the program and the fund release is limited to the official list submitted to the Field Office; and d) account for the excess children from the official list submitted per daycare centers and present with the municipal government for providing the appropriate fund needed as a local counterpart fund to the program.
observations.reason: The MSWDO justified that their feeding program depends significantly on the release of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) record transactions of the municipality by preparing appropriate adjusting journal entries to properly book the unrecorded receipts and disbursements of 2010; and (b) submit the disbursement vouchers to the Audit Team for post-audit.
observations.reason: There were balances in the financial statements which could not be traced to the source documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct local officials and employees to strictly follow the prescribed rules and regulations of Executive Order No. 298 and related circulars with respect to their travel expenditures.

We recommend that management submit the lacking documents and comply with requirements of Section 362 of COA Circular 91-368 whenever it undertakes minor or major repair in the maintenance of its service vehicles.
observations.reason: This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance as a result of post-audit.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant is advised not to process transactions that are not properly documented with the required supporting document. Other concerned officials are also advised not to approve and disburse the funds of the government without first satisfying the requirement set by the law.

We recommend that an inventory of land, land improvements and building be made and the results of the physical count be reconciled with its recorded valuation. Likewise, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card and Real Property Card should be maintained for each category of property and land, respectively. Further, these properties should be registered in the name of the Municipality with the Register of Deeds and duly supported with the proof of ownership such as certificate of land title and/or declaration of real properties.
